canonical_question: How do Bloom filters and probabilistic data structures work?
aliases: Counting Bloom filter vs Cuckoo filter
constraints: Standard Bloom filters do NOT support element deletion — removing bits may corrupt other entries
False positive rate increases as filter fills — never exceed designed capacity (n)
Hash functions must be independent and uniformly distributed — use murmur3/xxHash/FNV, not cryptographic hashes
A Bloom filter can never return false negatives — 'not in set' is always correct
Filter size (m) and hash count (k) are fixed at creation and cannot be changed after insertions begin
Thread safety is NOT built into most implementations — add synchronization for concurrent access

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need exact membership queries with zero false positives
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Hash set / hash table — deterministic O(1) lookup with no false positives

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need to count element frequencies, not just membership
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Count-Min Sketch — frequency estimation in sub-linear space

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need cardinality estimation (how many distinct elements)
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: HyperLogLog — distinct count in ~1.5 KB regardless of set size

inputs_needed.question: How many elements do you expect to insert into the filter?
What false positive rate is acceptable for your use case?
options: Not sure — recommend based on use case

inputs_needed.question: Do you need to delete elements from the filter?
options: Yes — need deletion support (Counting Bloom or Cuckoo filter)
